units.summary: vitest suite with HTTP mocked via nock; the strategy, integration, and variants subsets are the grade-runnable target.

units.title: Remediate src (6 findings)

findings.title: getInterval() can return a negative delay
findings.detail: It returns metricsInterval + failures*metricsInterval + a signed jitter; when metricsJitter exceeds metricsInterval the next setTimeout delay is negative and fires immediately, a tight resend loop. There is no clamp and no test at the boundary.

extra.consequence: It returns metricsInterval + failures*metricsInterval + a signed jitter; when metricsJitter exceeds metricsInterval the next setTimeout delay is negative and fires immediately, a tight resend loop. There is no clamp and no test at the boundary.

evidence: It returns metricsInterval + failures*metricsInterval + a signed jitter; when metricsJitter exceeds metricsInterval the next setTimeout delay is negative and fires immediately, a tight resend loop. There is no clamp and no test at the boundary.

findings.title: footgun: stop() clears the metrics timer with clearInterval
findings.detail: The sender reschedules itself with setTimeout (startTimer, line 175), so there is no interval to clear; Node clears either kind so it works today, but the name points a future fix at the wrong API.

extra.appearance: stop() clears the metrics timer with clearInterval
extra.reality: The sender reschedules itself with setTimeout (startTimer, line 175), so there is no interval to clear; Node clears either kind so it works today, but the name points a future fix at the wrong API.

evidence: stop() clears the metrics timer with clearInterval
The sender reschedules itself with setTimeout (startTimer, line 175), so there is no interval to clear; Node clears either kind so it works today, but the name points a future fix at the wrong API.

findings.title: Auto-start is fire-and-forget
findings.detail: The constructor runs process.nextTick(async () => this.start()) without awaiting or catching it; a repository.start() rejection becomes an unhandled promise rejection instead of an emitted Error event.

extra.consequence: The constructor runs process.nextTick(async () => this.start()) without awaiting or catching it; a repository.start() rejection becomes an unhandled promise rejection instead of an emitted Error event.

evidence: The constructor runs process.nextTick(async () => this.start()) without awaiting or catching it; a repository.start() rejection becomes an unhandled promise rejection instead of an emitted Error event.

findings.title: footgun: Variant carries both featureEnabled and feature_enabled
findings.detail: featureEnabled is deprecated but still set by hand at unleash.ts lines 313-314 alongside feature_enabled, so the two booleans can drift apart.

extra.appearance: Variant carries both featureEnabled and feature_enabled
extra.reality: featureEnabled is deprecated but still set by hand at unleash.ts lines 313-314 alongside feature_enabled, so the two booleans can drift apart.

evidence: Variant carries both featureEnabled and feature_enabled
featureEnabled is deprecated but still set by hand at unleash.ts lines 313-314 alongside feature_enabled, so the two booleans can drift apart.

units.summary: SDK facade and cross-cutting services: Unleash wires repository, client, and metrics and forwards their events; Metrics keeps bucketed counters and a self-rescheduling sender; request wraps make-fetch-happen GET/POST with agent selection; helpers and variant hold context resolution, instance id, jitter, and variant assignment.

units.title: Remediate src/strategy (72 findings)

findings.title: footgun: RemoteAddressStrategy reads as a general IP match but is IPv4-only
findings.detail: It constructs Address4 for both the range and the remote address; an IPv6 remoteAddress throws and is caught to false at line 22, unlike the dual-stack IN_CIDR operator in strategy.ts.

extra.appearance: RemoteAddressStrategy reads as a general IP match but is IPv4-only
extra.reality: It constructs Address4 for both the range and the remote address; an IPv6 remoteAddress throws and is caught to false at line 22, unlike the dual-stack IN_CIDR operator in strategy.ts.

evidence: RemoteAddressStrategy reads as a general IP match but is IPv4-only
It constructs Address4 for both the range and the remote address; an IPv6 remoteAddress throws and is caught to false at line 22, unlike the dual-stack IN_CIDR operator in strategy.ts.

findings.title: footgun: the strategy file is named remote-addresss-strategy (triple s)
findings.detail: strategy/index.ts line 7 imports that exact misspelling, so a well-meant rename to remote-address-strategy breaks the build.

extra.appearance: the strategy file is named remote-addresss-strategy (triple s)
extra.reality: strategy/index.ts line 7 imports that exact misspelling, so a well-meant rename to remote-address-strategy breaks the build.

evidence: the strategy file is named remote-addresss-strategy (triple s)
strategy/index.ts line 7 imports that exact misspelling, so a well-meant rename to remote-address-strategy breaks the build.

findings.title: silent failure: unparseable IP or CIDR matched to false
findings.detail: An invalid range or remote address is caught and the range check returns false, so a malformed IP rule silently never matches. Real: a typo in an IP rule disables it with no signal.

extra.swallowKind: unparseable IP or CIDR matched to false
extra.realVsBenign: An invalid range or remote address is caught and the range check returns false, so a malformed IP rule silently never matches. Real: a typo in an IP rule disables it with no signal.

evidence: unparseable IP or CIDR matched to false
An invalid range or remote address is caught and the range check returns false, so a malformed IP rule silently never matches. Real: a typo in an IP rule disables it with no signal.

findings.title: silent failure: invalid regex compiled to false
findings.detail: RegexOperator catches a compile error and evaluates the constraint false, so a misconfigured server-supplied pattern silently disables the flag instead of surfacing the error. Real: a bad pattern changes evaluation with no signal.

extra.realVsBenign: RegexOperator catches a compile error and evaluates the constraint false, so a misconfigured server-supplied pattern silently disables the flag instead of surfacing the error. Real: a bad pattern changes evaluation with no signal.

evidence: RegexOperator catches a compile error and evaluates the constraint false, so a misconfigured server-supplied pattern silently disables the flag instead of surfacing the error. Real: a bad pattern changes evaluation with no signal.

findings.title: silent failure: invalid semver compared to false
findings.detail: SemverOperator catches a parse error and evaluates false; same fail-closed-but-silent shape as the regex operator. Real but lower blast radius than regex.

extra.realVsBenign: SemverOperator catches a parse error and evaluates false; same fail-closed-but-silent shape as the regex operator. Real but lower blast radius than regex.

evidence: SemverOperator catches a parse error and evaluates false; same fail-closed-but-silent shape as the regex operator. Real but lower blast radius than regex.

findings.title: return values.some((range) => matchesRange(remoteAddress, range, Ad… → return undefined

extra.snippet: return values.some((range) => matchesRange(remoteAddress, range, Ad… → return undefined

evidence: return-undefined · return values.some((range) => matchesRange(remoteAddress, range, Ad… → return undefined

findings.title: return normalizedValue(id, groupId, normalizer, VARIANT_SEED) → return undefined

extra.snippet: return normalizedValue(id, groupId, normalizer, VARIANT_SEED) → return undefined

evidence: return-undefined · return normalizedValue(id, groupId, normalizer, VARIANT_SEED) → return undefined

units.summary: Feature-flag evaluation core: constraint operators (IN/STR/NUM/SEMVER/DATE/REGEX/CIDR) in strategy.ts, the Strategy base with getResult and variant selection, and the rollout strategies (flexible, gradual by user/session/random, application-hostname, remote-address, user-with-id).

units.title: Remediate src/repository (2 findings)

findings.title: An unknown response.type crashes the apply path
findings.detail: applyFeatureResponse routes an unknown top-level response.type to assertNever, which throws, reached from save() inside the fetcher callback; unknown delta events at line 262 only warn, so malformed server data is handled two different ways.

extra.consequence: applyFeatureResponse routes an unknown top-level response.type to assertNever, which throws, reached from save() inside the fetcher callback; unknown delta events at line 262 only warn, so malformed server data is handled two different ways.

evidence: applyFeatureResponse routes an unknown top-level response.type to assertNever, which throws, reached from save() inside the fetcher callback; unknown delta events at line 262 only warn, so malformed server data is handled two different ways.

findings.title: pruneOldFailures
findings.detail: reached through tested caller(s) (shouldFailover); no test exercises pruneOldFailures's no-op path directly

extra.title: pruneOldFailures() runs only via tested caller(s); no test exercises its no-op (trigger-not-met) path
extra.subject: pruneOldFailures
extra.detail: reached through tested caller(s) (shouldFailover); no test exercises pruneOldFailures's no-op path directly

evidence: reached through tested caller(s) (shouldFailover); no test exercises pruneOldFailures's no-op path directly

units.summary: Feature and segment store plus fetching: Repository holds the in-memory toggle map and loads backup then bootstrap; AdaptiveFetcher switches between PollingFetcher (HTTP) and StreamingFetcher (SSE); FailoverStrategy decides polling failover from a sliding window of failures.
